basics.name: Amir Mazaheri
basics.label: Computer Vision Research Scientist · PhD
basics.summary: Computer Vision scientist with deep expertise in large-scale video understanding, Vision-Language Models (VLMs), and multimodal AI systems. Pioneered state-of-the-art solutions across video temporal reasoning, content moderation, in-video search, and multimodal representation learning. PhD from UCF's CRCV. Publications at CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, EMNLP, and AAAI. Multiple US patents.

work.name: Warner Bros. Discovery (HBO)
work.position: Staff Machine Learning Engineer — Computer Vision
work.summary: Leading large-scale video understanding and content moderation systems, including automated harmful content detection and multi-modal safety classifiers for HBO's streaming platform. Developing temporal segmentation, scene/shot boundary detection, VLM-powered video indexing, and LLM-enhanced metadata generation for fine-grained video search and discovery.

work.name: Tubi
work.position: Senior Machine Learning Engineer — Computer Vision
work.summary: AI-driven content analysis and personalization using VLMs: brand identification, logo detection, scene segmentation, personalized trailers, content artwork generation, and LLM-powered recommendation enhancements.

work.name: Aibee U.S. Corporation
work.summary: CV models for human tracking in crowds, person re-identification, and AI-based benchmarking tools for tracking.

work.name: Netflix
work.summary: Semi-supervised activity detection for movies; attentive deep neural network for movie billboard design optimization.

work.name: Nielsen
work.summary: Automated TV advertisement analysis: classification, tagging, and descriptive analysis of broadcast ads.

work.name: Center for Research in Computer Vision (CRCV), University of Central Florida
work.summary: PhD research with Prof. Mubarak Shah on video understanding, visual question answering, Visual Text Correction, video generation, and multimodal AI.

education.score: Dissertation: Video Content Understanding Using Text. Advisor: Prof. Mubarak Shah, CRCV.

publications.name: WoundNet: A Domain-Adaptable Few-Shot Classification Framework for Wound Healing Assessment
Context-Aware Analysis of Group Submissions for Group Anomaly Detection and Performance Prediction
Video Generation from Text Employing Latent Path Construction for Temporal Modeling
MMFT-BERT: Multimodal Fusion Transformer with BERT Encodings for Visual Question Answering
Pay Attention! — Robustifying a Deep Visuomotor Policy through Task-Focused Attention
Learning a Multi-concept Video Retrieval Model with Multiple Latent Variables
Video Fill In the Blank using LR/RL LSTMs with Spatial-Temporal Attentions
